methodology: First eval per skill. 4 assertions derived from expected_output. Strict pass/fail grading of simulated answer grounded only in the respective skill version.

skills.name: api-validation
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I have a Node.js API at localhost:3000 with /api/posts endpoints. I need to validate the full CRUD cycle (create, read, update, delete) and capture evidence.
assertions: Health check first: curl with tee/jq saved to e2e-evidence (e.g. api-health.json)
CRUD sequence covered: create -> extract ID -> read -> update -> verify persisted -> delete -> verify 404
Every response body saved as evidence (JSON body + status code, not just status)
Empty response body / bare 200 is flagged as FAIL / suspicious

old_skill.key_miss: None — snapshot already had full CRUD cycle, ID extraction, tee patterns, and empty-body flag.

with_skill.key_miss: None — content preserved and augmented with quick-start table + crud-validator.sh fast path.

skills.name: ai-evidence-analysis
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I captured 3 pieces of evidence for my login journey: login-page.png, auth-response.json, and build-log.txt. I want AI to analyze all 3 and tell me if they all support a PASS verdict.
assertions: Produces per-evidence-type confidence scores with type-specific score bands (screenshot/api/log)
Describes aggregation from per-item scores into a journey-level confidence score
States that if all items are 70+, journey score lands ~75+ (PASS threshold)
A CRITICAL finding forces the verdict to FAIL regardless of score

old_skill.key_miss: No per-type score bands, no journey-level aggregation formula, no journey-threshold guidance. Only the CRITICAL=>FAIL rule was inline.

with_skill.key_miss: Journey-level 70+ -> 75+ PASS threshold is not stated inline — but references/confidence-scoring.md is explicitly named with aggregation + per-type rubric, which an agent would load to answer.

skills.name: e2e-validate
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I have a project that's a React frontend + Node.js backend in the same repo. /validate says it doesn't know what platform to validate.
assertions: Auto-detects fullstack when both frontend and backend routes are present
Provides a --platform fullstack override flag
Guides bottom-up order: DB -> API (curl) -> frontend UI
Each layer should be independently verifiable so failures can be isolated

old_skill.key_miss: Override flag present as generic --platform <type> but no explicit fullstack example; layer-independence rationale implicit, not stated.

with_skill.key_miss: None — new 'When signals conflict' table explicitly covers Next.js-with-API-routes -> fullstack, 'override with --platform <type> and re-run' is explicit, and 'Why bottom-up?' + per-platform breakdown covers layer independence.

skills.name: forge-setup
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: First-time setup on a monorepo with React frontend + Express API + iOS native. Running /forge-setup.
assertions: Detects web (package.json + React), api (Express), ios (.xcodeproj)
Recommends/defaults to standard enforcement
Creates .validationforge/config.json with the platforms array + scaffolds e2e-evidence/
Prints setup summary line: Platforms / Enforcement / Evidence / Rules: 8 installed / MCP status

old_skill.key_miss: None — snapshot already had detection table, standard default, config schema, and output block.

with_skill.key_miss: None — same content, richer rule-by-rule description table + verification fix column + forge-init.sh automation hint.

skills.name: ios-validation
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I have an iOS app in Xcode. It builds fine, but when I try to validate it on simulator, xcodebuild fails with 'No scheme found'.
assertions: Run xcodebuild -list to enumerate available schemes
Explain that a missing scheme means it isn't registered in the .xcodeproj
Fix path: add scheme via Xcode Product -> Scheme -> Edit Scheme, or xcodebuild -scheme with exact name
Retry the Build step after adding the scheme

old_skill.key_miss: Does not mention the Xcode UI path (Product -> Scheme -> Edit Scheme). Only tells you to run xcodebuild -list.

with_skill.key_miss: Same Xcode-UI gap. Pass-1 additions were fast-path script + when-to-use framing; no new scheme-editing guidance.

skills.name: no-mocking-validation-gates
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I'm validating a React component that calls an external payment API. The API is slow and flaky in staging. Should I mock it or stub the response?
assertions: Diagnose why the real system is unavailable (slow/flaky staging)
Propose concrete fixes (fix flakiness, production-like data, caching)
Redirect to real-system validation
Reference the Three-Step DIAGNOSE -> FIX -> VERIFY correction protocol

old_skill.key_miss: No concrete fix proposals (caching, production-like data) — only generic 'fix it / make available'.

with_skill.key_miss: Same: new version adds the 'users -> data field rename' real-world example which is vivid but still no caching / slow-API mitigation menu. Three-step protocol and references/real-system-validation-guide.md still linked.

summary.floor_effect_note: api-validation and forge-setup were already at 1.0 in the snapshot; no room for improvement against eval 1.
